title: MongoDB Atomicity - Complete Guide
description: Comprehensive guide on atomicity and transactions in MongoDB for ACID compliance

atomicity_overview.definition: Atomicity ensures operations complete entirely or not at all - no partial updates
purpose: Multi-step operations succeed together or fail together
atomicity_overview.key_concept: Either ALL changes succeed OR none of them do - never partial

section_1_document_level_atomicity.title: Document-Level Atomicity (Single Document)
section_1_document_level_atomicity.definition: Operations on single document are always atomic
section_1_document_level_atomicity.guarantee: Update entire document or update nothing
section_1_document_level_atomicity.benefit: Simple, no locks needed, always safe
section_1_document_level_atomicity.atomicity_guarantee: No partial updates - either entire operation succeeds or fails completely

section_2_multi_document_atomicity.title: Multi-Document Atomicity (Transactions)
section_2_multi_document_atomicity.definition: ACID transactions for multiple documents across one or more collections
section_2_multi_document_atomicity.benefit: All documents updated together or none

steps: Reduce scholarship of student A by 5000
Increase scholarship of student B by 5000
example_scenario.without_transaction: Step 1 succeeds, step 2 fails → inconsistent state
example_scenario.with_transaction: All succeed or all fail → consistent state

section_3_transaction_example.title: Complete Transaction Example
section_3_transaction_example.description: Transfer 5000 from student A to student B scholarship
section_3_transaction_example.atomicity: All 3 operations succeed together or fail together

failure_scenarios.scenario: Student A update succeeds, Student B update fails
failure_scenarios.without_transaction: A loses 5000, B doesn't gain → INCONSISTENT
failure_scenarios.with_transaction: ROLLBACK: A gets money back, no inconsistency

failure_scenarios.without_transaction: Money transferred but no record → AUDIT FAIL

atomicity.guarantee: No partial updates, no inconsistent state

consistency.definition: Database valid before and after transaction
consistency.example: Total scholarships always accurate, no negative amounts

durability.mechanism: Write to journal and replica set
durability.assurance: Data persists even if server crashes

example_1_single_document.guarantee: GPA updated completely or not at all

example_2_single_document_array.scenario: Add skill to student skills array
example_2_single_document_array.guarantee: Skill added to array or not

example_3_multi_document_complex.scenario: Grade entry: update student GPA and insert grade record
example_3_multi_document_complex.failure_protection: If grade insert fails, GPA update rolled back

example_4_scholarship_transfer.scenario: Transfer scholarship from one student to another
example_4_scholarship_transfer.atomicity: All 3 operations succeed together or all rolled back
example_4_scholarship_transfer.protection: Money doesn't disappear, both accounts stay consistent

example_5_bulk_enrollment.scenario: Enroll student in course (update student + create enrollment record)
example_5_bulk_enrollment.atomicity: Course added to both student and enrollment records atomically

levels.description: See uncommitted changes from other transactions
Same data read multiple times within transaction
levels.use: Prevents dirty reads and non-repeatable reads

usecase_2_inventory_update.title: Inventory Management
usecase_2_inventory_update.scenario: Sell item: reduce stock + create order

usecase_3_grade_entry.title: Grade Entry
usecase_3_grade_entry.scenario: Record grade and update student GPA

usecase_4_enrollment.title: Course Enrollment
usecase_4_enrollment.requirement: Student and enrollment records in sync

practice_1_prefer_single_document.practice: Design schema to keep related data in single document
practice_1_prefer_single_document.why: Avoid transaction overhead, atomic by default
practice_1_prefer_single_document.example: Store scholarshipDetails inside student document, not separate

practice_2_transactions_for_complex.practice: Use transactions only for complex multi-step operations
practice_2_transactions_for_complex.why: Transactions have overhead, use only when needed
practice_2_transactions_for_complex.example: Use for financial transfer, not simple updates

practice_3_keep_transactions_short.practice: Keep transaction scope small and duration short
practice_3_keep_transactions_short.why: Locks held during transaction, long locks impact concurrency
practice_3_keep_transactions_short.example: Don't fetch large datasets inside transaction

practice_4_error_handling.why: Ensure transaction rolled back on error

practice_5_write_concern_with_transactio.why: Ensure transaction durability across replica set

practice_6_test_failure_scenarios.practice: Test transaction behavior when one operation fails

mistake_1_expecting_atomicity_without_tr.mistake: Two separate updateOne calls, expecting both or neither

mistake_2_long_running_transactions.problem: Locks held for long time, blocks other operations
mistake_2_long_running_transactions.solution: Keep transactions short, fetch data before transaction

mistake_3_transactions_on_standalone.solution: Use replica set or revert to single-document design

mistake_4_no_error_handling.mistake: Not catching errors or rolling back

section_11_when_to_use_atomicity.title: When to Use Transactions
use_transactions_when: Multiple documents must be updated together
Failure of one operation should rollback others
dont_use_transactions_when: Single document update (use atomic by default)

section_13_rollback_example.title: Rollback Behavior Example
section_13_rollback_example.scenario: Student already has scholarship, duplicate check fails

code.step3: Check if student B already has scholarship: find (DUPLICATE FOUND)
code.result: Step 2 is ROLLED BACK - student A gets money back

section_13_rollback_example.outcome: Both students end with original amounts - atomic

types: Single document: Always atomic by default
summary.key_benefit: Prevents inconsistent state, guarantees data integrity
summary.when_to_use: Use single-document by default, transactions for complex multi-step
summary.best_practice: Design schema to keep related data together for atomicity
summary.acid: Atomicity is one part of ACID guarantee
